strategy_reviews.edge_mechanism: price dislocates away from fast value, reclaims it, and the position scales in only when the reclaim improves on the next bar
strategy_reviews.hypothesis: ETH 1h is the primary lane and sits in a chop-to-transition mix. That should produce repeated failed extensions and value recoveries, but the all-in first reclaim is fragile. A scale-in only after the reclaim proves itself gives denser participation without the ceremonial sparsity that killed earlier cycles.

strategy_reviews.edge_mechanism: a continuation trigger is taken only after directional pressure reasserts above fast structure, while stop logic and exit logic are intentionally separated to create asymmetry
strategy_reviews.hypothesis: The cycle explicitly asks for continuation with asymmetric exit logic where trigger, risk, and exit components play different roles. BTC 1h is the best-fit lane because it carries more directional transition quality than ETH 1h while staying liquid enough to test whether separated exit logic improves payoff asymmetry.

AXS 1h is the best structurally different lane for this because it is expressive enough for visible transitions and should produce denser retest events than the 4h lanes that kept failing. learning_requirements: Every cycle must yield diagnosis, lesson extraction, and durable learning update.
Zero-trade outcomes require explicit diagnosis and one rescue attempt at most.
Only three terminal decisions are legal: pass, refine, abort.
PASS should flow into structured refinement variants, not cosmetic tweaks.
